payload.description: SBS route on Nostrand Avenue/Rogers Avenue/Bedford Avenue in Brooklyn, from Williamsburg Bridge Bus Plaza to Emmons Avenue/Knapp Street
B44 local bus service on Nostrand Avenue and New York Avenue in Brooklyn
Predecessor limited-stop service on Nostrand Avenue and New York Avenue, replaced by B44 SBS in November 2013
B49 local bus operating on Rogers Avenue and Bedford Avenue in Brooklyn

payload.project_name: B44 Select Bus Service on Nostrand Avenue
payload.description: Fifth and final Phase I SBS route. Implemented November 2013. Joint NYCT and NYC DOT project.
North-south corridor in Brooklyn, carries B44 SBS and B44 Local. Vision Zero priority corridor.
Northbound SBS and B49 corridor north of Flatbush Avenue. Wider street with bus lanes and bus bulbs. Vision Zero priority corridor.
Former B44 Limited northbound route. Narrow two-way street. B44 Local continues to serve it northbound.

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Design and Construction

payload.claim_text: Introduce a new, high performance transit option for NYC by improving the speed, reliability, and attractiveness of the B44
Provide measurable benefits to existing B44 users and attract new riders
Increase street safety across all modes on the project corridors
Minimize impact to vehicular traffic flow on project corridors, and more efficiently utilize street space for motorists, transit riders, and pedestrians

payload.description: B44 Select Bus Service launched
Final completion of Flushing Avenue to Atlantic Avenue street reconstruction (DDC)
Bus Time information made available throughout Brooklyn including B44 SBS
Press conference introducing B44 SBS with MTA Chairman Tom Prendergast, Mayor Michael Bloomberg, DOT Commissioner Janette Sadik-Khan, MTA Bus President Darryl Irick, NYCT President Carmen Bianco

9.6 miles of bus lanes implemented along B44 SRS counting both directions, covering just over half the route. Offset bus lane configuration on Nostrand Avenue; curbside bus lanes where space constrained.
Off-board fare collection with MetroCard and coin machines at every bus stop. Roving fare inspectors check tickets on board.
15 bus bulbs constructed — eight on Nostrand Avenue and seven on Rogers and Bedford Avenues. All north of Flatbush Avenue. High curb nearly level with bus floor.
5 neckdowns (curb extensions at intersection crosswalks) installed at locations identified by school safety studies
62-foot long, 3-door articulated buses replaced 40-foot, 2-door buses for B44 SBS — first use of such buses in Brooklyn. 40-foot, 2-door buses continue on B44 Local.
Northbound B44 SBS rerouted from New York Avenue to Rogers Avenue, decreasing northbound route from 9.9 to 9.5 miles. B44 Local maintained on New York Avenue.
Real-time bus arrival signs. One installed at Church Avenue for launch. At least 18 more planned on Nostrand, Rogers, and Bedford Avenues in 2016.

Northbound PM: 20% faster
Southbound AM: 19% faster
Southbound PM: 15% faster

Northbound main bus lane section PM: -33%
Southbound Nostrand at Flatbush to Knapp St at Shore Pkwy AM: -29%
Southbound Nostrand at Flatbush to Knapp St at Shore Pkwy PM: -35%
10% B44 SBS ridership increase from 2014 to 2015
1% decline in overall Brooklyn ridership 2014 to 2015
2% reduction in traffic injuries where bus lanes were added
37% reduction in traffic injuries where crossing distances were shortened with bus bulbs or neckdowns
15-28 minutes saved for commuters traveling the majority of the route in either direction
B49 northbound in bus lane section AM: -7% (26.5 to 24.6 min)
B49 northbound in bus lane section PM: -11% (30 to 26.7 min)
2014 Averages: B44 SBS 17,249
2014 Averages: B44 local 18,767
2015 Averages: B44 local 18,035 (-4%)
Prior to SBS, B44 traveled between 6.7 and 7.2 miles per hour on average
13.6 million annual riders historically
Historically carried between 35,000 and 40,000 people on an average weekday
Average of 583 traffic injuries per year in two years before implementation between Atlantic and Flatbush on Nostrand and Rogers Avenues
46% of B44 SBS riders were very satisfied
14% of B44 Limited riders were very satisfied (pre-SBS)
18% of those surveyed being new to the B44
B44 Limited: 25.8 min stopped at bus stops
B44 SBS: 15.4 min stopped at bus stops
B44 Limited Total: 83.2 min
B44 SBS Total: 65.2 min
AM Peak NB: Pre 19.0 min, Post 19.8 min (+4%)
PM Peak NB: Pre 18.3 min, Post 16.7 min (-9%)
53% of merchants were concerned about deliveries
75% were concerned about customer parking
Average of 84% of shoppers came to Nostrand Avenue by walking or taking the bus/subway
11% of Nostrand Avenue shoppers arrived by car

payload.description: Gates Avenue and Avenue L SBS stations added post-launch in response to public feedback
Williamsburg Bridge Bus Plaza reconstruction by DDC. Substantially complete as of April 2016. NYCT indoor waiting area and crew facility expected summer 2016.

payload.claim_text: Speeding prevalent in AM and PM peaks on Rogers Avenue. DOT restoring PM parking and some AM parking on Rogers Avenue between Beverly Road and Maple Street to narrow roadway and lessen speeding opportunities.
67% expressed interest in a delivery zone either directly in front of their store, across the street, or around the corner. 88% of deliveries take less than 30 minutes to complete.
A 10 AM – 4 PM window for deliveries, truck loading, or customer parking. Delivery window typically 10 AM – 12 PM on east side, 12 – 2 PM on west side.

payload.claim_text: The project plan qualified as a Type II action under local (CEQR) guidelines and as a Categorical Exclusion under federal (NEPA) guidelines.

payload.raw_value_text: Between May 2014 and May 2015, B44 SBS ridership at Avenue D and Church Avenue increased 37%

payload.claim_text: The B44 was the fifth and final Phase I SBS route to be implemented, the 6th SBS route overall, and is now one of nine SBS routes in the city. The first four Phase I corridors (Bx12, M15, M34, & S79) were implemented between 2008 and 2012.

Select Bus Service (SBS) is a joint program between MTA New York City Transit (NYCT) and the New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT), which aims to improve transit service through the application of Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) principles to New York City's busiest bus routes.

The B44 is the 3rd highest ridership bus route in Brooklyn, and the 6th highest in all of New York City. Approximately 60% of residents within 1/4 mile of the service commute by transit, and more than half of households do not own a car.
For the November 2013 launch, one real-time information sign was installed at the Church Avenue stop on Nostrand Avenue.
NYC DOT is planning to install at least 18 additional real-time bus arrival signs on Nostrand, Rogers, and Bedford Avenues in 2016.

payload.raw_value_text: Pre-SBS NB AM: 89 min
Post-SBS NB AM: 61.2 min
Pre-SBS NB PM: 83.1 min
Post-SBS NB PM: 66.7 min
Between May 2014 and May 2015, B49 ridership at Avenue D and Church Avenue increased 47%
Total northbound ridership at Avenue D and Church Avenue increased 22% between May 2014 and May 2015
Total northbound ridership at Avenue D and Church Avenue is approximately 3% higher than pre-SBS levels
In the last 4 years, 68 pedestrians were killed or seriously injured on Nostrand Avenue, and 22 on Rogers Avenue between Dean Street and Farragut Road
B44 Limited: 37.4 min bus in motion (45% of total)
B44 SBS: 37.3 min bus in motion (57% of total)

payload.claim_text: NYCT interviewed 1,186 shoppers on Nostrand Avenue in the summer of 2011 near Glenwood Road, Church Avenue, Empire Boulevard, and Fulton Street.

192 storefront businesses surveyed along Nostrand Avenue between Eastern Parkway and Linden Boulevard. 149 additional businesses surveyed by mail on Nostrand, Rogers, and Bedford Avenues between Flushing Avenue and Avenue I. Summer 2011.
Signal timing changes: northbound-southbound green time added at Linden Boulevard; coordination of signals approaching Eastern Parkway improved.
